text: Users should revisit the terms periodically, although in case of material changes, the service will notify

explanation: The service may update the Terms without notice, but if they significantly change the agreements in a manner that importantly and/or negatively affects the way the service is used, users will be notified.

text: This service does not sell your personal data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: This service makes an explicit promise not to sell "personal data" of users.

text: This service assumes no liability for any losses or damages resulting from any matter relating to the service
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service assumes no liability for any damages the user incurs, including tangible (e.g. profits) and intangible losses (e.g. data), resulting from the use or inability to use the service or any other matter relating to the service.

text: IP addresses of website visitors are not tracked
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Service disclaims that IP adresses from users are not collected (or anonymized) nor shared.

text: The service will resist legal requests for user information where reasonably possible
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service will attempt to protect user information to a great extent, even so far as stating that they will only follow legal requirements to disclose user information in circumstances where this cannot be reasonably resisted.

text: The service provider makes no warranty regarding uninterrupted, timely, secure or error-free service
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service provider expressly does not claim to provide uninterrupted, timely, secure or error-free service.

text: This service does not track you
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The document excerpt needs to include the words "[the service] does not track [users]" or "[the service] does not use any tracking technology".

text: The service is provided 'as is' and to be used at the users' sole risk
label: potentially_fair
explanation: This service does not provide any guarantees as to its usability or fitness for the users' purposes. Users agree to use it at their own risk, accepting any possible bugs, malfunctions or harm to their devices.

text: Users agree not to submit libelous, harassing or threatening content
label: potentially_fair
explanation: This service prohibits users from submitting content of a variety of types. This can include content that is unlawful, harassing, abusive, threatening, libelous, sexist, racist, homophobic, transphobic, or discriminatory in any other way.

text: Your personal data is used for limited purposes
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Your Personal Data is only used for the essential purposes of the Service, which are clearly defined on the Agreements. For instance, the Service won't abuse your data to deliver targeted advertisements to you or for marketing purposes.

text: You have the right to leave this service at any time
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You can stop using the service and/or cancel or delete your account at any time for any reason. Moreover, the termination is reasonably fast and simple.

text: This service does not guarantee that it or the products obtained through it meet the users' expectations or requirements
label: potentially_fair
explanation: This service provider informs its users that the services, information, products, or other materials provided on its website may not meet the users' expectations or requirements.

text: User-generated content is encrypted, and this service cannot decrypt it
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Content generated by the users is end-to-end encrypted, in a way that it can't be accessed by anyone unauthorised, and even the service has no technical means to decrypt it.

text: The service does not use third-party analytics or tracking platforms
label: clearly_fair
explanation: There are no Google Analytics or other tracking codes embedded in the service.  Any tracking is performed by the service itself.

text: Your personal data is aggregated into statistics
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Your personal data is used for statistics in a de-identified/aggregated form (i.e. not directly linked to a unique identity) to analyse trends, for research and for analytics.

text: The terms for this service are translated into different languages
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Although there's generally a unique binding language for the terms, they are translated to allow all users to understand the agreement they're accepting, no matter their native language.

text: This service provides an onion site accessible over Tor
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Tor is a software for anonymous communication that directs Internet traffic through an overlay network. Onion sites are only available through Tor browser and provide additional security and anonymity to users.

text: The service can delete your account without prior notice and without a reason
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: At any time, your account can be terminated without explanation and without warning by the service.

text: The service does not share user information with third parties
label: clearly_fair

text: This service uses third-party cookies for statistics
label: potentially_unfair

text: The service may keep a secure, anonymized record of users' data for analytical purposes even after the data retention period
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The data retention period states how long a service keeps user data. In this case, the service may anonymize the personal data after its retention period is over and use it for analytical or research purposes.

text: There is a date of the last update of the terms

label: clearly_fair

Since the arbitrator can decide the outgoing of a dispute without evidences or a legal basis, it is preferable that users decide whether they want or not their dispute to be resolved this way.

text: You waive your right to a class action.
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The right to be represented in a class action (or representative action) exists in the US and some European countries. It allows people who have been similarly injured by the same accused to take legal action by a single lawsuit instead of individually. Some Terms of Service try to limit that right. By agreeing to them, you could waive (= renounce to) your right to a class action, which might reduce your chances to be successful in a case.

text: You waive your moral rights
label: clearly_unfair
explanation:
Moral rights are rights to creators of literary, dramatic, musical and artistic works, as well as directors of films.

"The moral rights include the right of attribution, the right to have a work published anonymously or pseudonymously, and the right to the integrity of the work. The preserving of the integrity of the work allows the author to object to alteration, distortion, or mutilation of the work that is "prejudicial to the author's honor or reputation". Anything else that may detract from the artist's relationship with the work even after it leaves the artist's possession or ownership may bring these moral rights into play. Moral rights are distinct from any economic rights tied to copyrights. Even if an artist has assigned his or her copyright rights to a work to a third party, he or she still maintains the moral rights to the work." -Wikipedia

In general, any terms that use the exact words "moral rights" as rights that are waived will fall under this Case.

text: Tracking pixels are used in service-to-user communication
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service tracks the user count or some actions of the user; this is usually used for statistics. For example, emails sent by a service can contain a pixel tag, which is a small, clear image that can tell them whether or not you have opened an email and what your IP address is.

text: Users who have been permanently banned from this service are not allowed to re-register under a new account
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Once users have been permanently banned from this service, they are not allowed to create a new account to keep using the service.

text: You are entitled to a refund if certain thresholds or standards are not met by the service
label: clearly_fair

text: You agree not to use the service for illegal purposes
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The user agrees not to commit any unlawful acts through the use of the website, including the posting of illegal content or messaging.

text: 30 days of notice are given before closing your account
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Terms explain they will notify users at least 30 days before closing their accounts, which allows users to limit the losses and damages caused by this termination.

text: Your personal data may be disclosed to comply with government requests without notice to you
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: If the Government governing the Terms requests personal data of a user of this service, they may not inform/notify the user while complying with the request.

text: No need to register
label: clearly_fair
explanation: This service can be used without providing a user profile.

text: Your private content may be accessed by people working for the service
label: potentially_fair

text: This service collects your IP address, which can be used to view your approximate location
label: potentially_fair

text: Users shall not interfere with another person's enjoyment of the service
label: potentially_unfair
explanation:
Users are asked not to interfere with another person't enjoyment in using the service.
However, this statement is very vague and open to interpretation by the service itself.

text: The user is informed about security practices
label: clearly_fair
explanation: It's important to know what security measures are taken to protect your data from unauthorized access. This case applies to the Agreements that precisely explain their security practices.

text: This service has a no refund policy with some exceptions
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Payments can be refunded if some requirements are fulfilled.

text: This service prohibits users sending chain letters, junk mail, spam or any unsolicited messages.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users are not allowed to use the service for chain letters, junk mail, spamming or any other distribution of content to people who have not agreed to receiving it.

text: Prohibits the posting of pornographic content
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users are not allowed to post any content of pornographic nature. This includes adult content, things that are sexually explicit, or obscene.

text: This service assumes no liability for unauthorized access to your personal information
label: potentially_unfair

text: Features of the website are made available under a free software license
label: clearly_fair
explanation: This case applies to services under licenses that are free as in "freedom". According to the Free Software Foundation, "free software is the software that grants the user the freedom to share, study, and modify it". Furthermore, the code is entirely open source and may be used without any restriction, including for commercial purposes.

text: Any liability on behalf of the service is only limited to the fees you paid as a user
label: potentially_unfair

text: Provides instructions on how to submit a copyright claim
label: potentially_fair
explanation: If content that you own is copied or made accessible through the service in a way that constitutes an intellectual property right infringement, you can report it following the instructions given in the agreements.

text: Many third parties are involved in operating the service
label: potentially_unfair

text: The service may use web beacons and device fingerprinting on users.
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Tracking technologies (such as web beacons, tracking pixels...) are employed on users and/or the service assigns a unique ID to a browser or device to track its behaviour.

text: You are solely responsible for claims made against the service and agree to indemnify the service.
label: potentially_fair
explanation:
You are responsible for your use of the service. If a claim arises related to that use, you agree to indemnify and hold the service harmless.
If users are also required to defend the service, see Case 146 instead.

text: The court of law governing the terms is in New Zealand
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction (specified in the title of each point by replacing the "X").

text: User accounts can be terminated after having been in breach of the terms of service repeatedly
label: clearly_fair
explanation: User accounts can be terminated for having been in breach of the terms of service repeatedly, meaning they cannot be terminated without a reason, but only for a limited number of reasons. Additionally, user accounts will not be deleted after a first infringement.

text: Copyright license limited for the purposes of that same service
label: clearly_fair

text: When the service wants to change its terms, users are notified a week or more in advance.
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users will be notified a week before the Terms are somehow updated, which lets them a reasonable amount of time to carefully read the changes and perhaps stop using the services if they do not longer agree.

text: The service can suspend your account for several reasons
label: potentially_fair

text: You can request access, correction and/or deletion of your data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users have the right to access personal data hold by the Service, correct it and/or permanently delete it. This usually applies to Services GDPR compliants.

text: This service prohibits users from attempting to gain unauthorized access to other computer systems
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service does not allow any attempts at hacking or otherwise gaining unauthorized access to other users' computers.

text: If you are the target of a copyright holder's take down notice, this service gives you the opportunity to defend yourself
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You have the right to send a counter-notice to the service if you believe the content that was removed wasn't infringing any copyright.

text: Tracking via third-party cookies for advertising
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service's website sets third-party cookies (cookies that do not belong to their domain) for purposes not including statistics and analytics.

text: You can retrieve an archive of your data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users can request a copy of the data collected through the services and infer from it the amount of personal information that is processed and stored by the Service. This usually applies to Services GDPR compliants.

text: You are being tracked via social media cookies/pixels
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: These types of cookies/pixels send information to social media sites when you interact with the service.

text: This service tracks which web page referred you to it
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service logs the URL of the last web page you visited before visiting the Site and may use that information to gather more data about you.

text: You can request access, correction and deletion of your data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users have the right to access personal data hold by the Service, correct it and/or permanently delete it. This usually applies to Services GDPR compliants.

text: The data retention period is kept to the minimum necessary for fulfilling its purposes
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service will remove personal data from its systems once it is no longer required. Depending on the type of information and the reason it was collected, data retention periods will vary.

text: Your personal data may be used for marketing purposes
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service may use your personal data for marketing, such as sending you personalised offerings.

text: Your personal data is used to employ targeted third-party advertising
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Your Personal Information is traded and shared with advertising companies to target ads on you, either on the website you were visiting or in third party websites. Your interaction with the service and data you have shared with them may be gathered to create an advertising ID or profile.

text: Your personal data is used for advertising
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Your interaction with the service and data you have shared with them may be gathered to create an advertising ID or profile to target ads on you, either on the website you were visiting or in third party websites.

text: Prices and fees may be changed at any time, without notice to you
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service reserves the right to change or modify its pricing and fees at any time, and such changes are effective immediately without need for further notice to the user.

text: Personal information that is shared outside its jurisdiction is processed according to the original jurisdiction’s data protection standards
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service has arrangements in place to ensure that users' personal information, if shared with an entity outside of the service's jurisdiction, is processed to the same standards that apply to the processing of personal data in the service's jurisdiction.

text: The court of law governing the terms is in a jurisdiction that is friendlier to user privacy protection (Belgium)
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction known for protecting citizens privacy with strong data regulations. Usually contributors will need to explain in the Analysis view of each point linked to this Case why the jurisdiction is friendly to user privacy protection.

text: This service may collect, use, and share location data
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Location-based information is collected and may be used and/or shared with third parties for purposes not directly related to the services provided (i.e. the Service doesn't rely on the use or share of location data to function properly).

text: You are not allowed to use a nick name
label: potentially_fair
explanation: You must provide your legal name and pseudonyms aren't allowed, but it makes sense for the purposes of this service.

text: The copyright license on user data is broader than necessary
label: potentially_unfair

text: Couchsurfing may share aggregated or anonymized information that does not directly identify you.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Your personal data is used for statistics in a de-identified/aggregated form (i.e. not directly linked to a unique identity) to analyse trends, for research and for analytics.

text: This service is only available to users over 18.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Services are intended for users who are at least a certain age. If you are younger than the age required then you are prohibited from using the Service.

text: Prohibits political discussions or campaigning
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Political content in the form or pictures, discussions, or campaigning isn't allowed.

text: This service does not allow alternative accounts
label: potentially_fair
explanation:
Services that do not allow multiple or alternative accounts are covered by this case.
Alternative accounts can contribute to an increased privacy, but can also be used to manipulate discussions and polls and might decrease the trustworthiness of a person.

text: Prohibits public posting of private messages
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service does not allow users to publicly post private messages they have received from other users.

text: The service can read your private messages
label: clearly_unfair
explanation: The Service is able to access, scan, review or read all user communications, including private messages, which may contain personal or sensitive data.

text: Couchsurfing informs you about the fact that you can disable cookies
label: potentially_unfair

text: The service can delete specific content without prior notice and without a reason
label: clearly_unfair
explanation: This service can delete specific content. Usually they will claim they reserve the right to do it at their sole discretion, which is legalese for saying they won't give a specific reason. If the terms say they will not notify you, it shall be included in the quote but if it does not explicitly say they will give you a notice, it can be reasonably inferred that they can do it without telling you.

text: You cannot blame CouchSurfing for disputes you have with other users
label: potentially_fair
explanation: You are responsible for the interactions and the disputes that may occur with other users of the Service. In the event of a dispute, you release the Service from all claims resulting from it.

text: Couchsurfing becomes the owner of ideas you give them
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service can claim ownership on feedback, proposals, ideas and suggestions submitted by the users, who may waive their "moral rights" on that feedback.

text: Couchsurfing may retain your data after deactivation for legitimate business purposes
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service may still use your content and display it after you stopped using it by closing your account, since you granted them a perpetual or (in some cases) an unlimited license over that content.

text: This service prohibits users sending chain letters, junk mail, spam or any unsolicited messages
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users are not allowed to use the service for chain letters, junk mail, spamming or any other distribution of content to people who have not agreed to receiving it.

text: You should follow the community guidelines
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Use of the service is subject also to an exhaustive list of other documents containing rules, guidelines, terms or policies (in addition to the obvious Terms of Service/Terms of Use and Privacy Policy/Data Use Policy)

text: Couchsurfing may close your account at their sole discretion
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: At any time, your account can be terminated without explanation and without warning by the service.

text: Prohibits the posting of content which promotes or glorifies violence or politically or religiously extremist values.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users are not allowed to post extremist political or religious content. They are also not allowed to post content that glorifies violence.

text: Couchsurfing keeps the license on your content, even after you close your account
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service may still use your content and display it after you stopped using it by closing your account. The case applies to terms requiring users to grant them a perpetual license over that content.

text: This service can license user content to third parties
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: By posting content through the Services, users grant them a transferable and/or sublicensable right, which allows user content to be licensed to third parties.

text: When the service wants to make a material change to its terms, users are notified at least 30 days in advance
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service may update the Terms without notice, but if they significantly change the agreements in a manner that importantly and/or negatively affects the way the service is used, users will be notified at least 30 days in advance.

text: The service may collect extra data about you through promotions
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Additional personal data may be collected through contests, surveys, promotions, petitions or sweepstakes.

text: The court of law governing the terms is State of California (U.S.)
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction (specified in the title of each point by replacing the "X").

text: This service keeps a license on user-generated content even after users close their accounts.
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service may still use your content and display it after you stopped using it by closing your account. The case applies to terms requiring users to grant them a perpetual license over that content.

text: Tracking via third-party cookies for purposes including advertising
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service's website sets third-party cookies (cookies that do not belong to their domain) for purposes not including statistics and analytics.

text: The service may sell your data unless you opt out
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: There is a form to opt-out (typically California's CCPA) of the selling of your personal data. This is a bad a point because there are many services that don't have this form due to not selling your data in the first place.

text: You can get your data back
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users can request a copy of the data collected through the services and infer from it the amount of personal information that is processed and stored by the Service. This usually applies to Services GDPR compliants.

text: Right to leave the service
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You can stop using the service and/or cancel or delete your account at any time for any reason. Moreover, the termination is reasonably fast and simple.

text: You maintain ownership of your data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: This service does not claim ownership over user-generated content or materials, and the user doesn't need to waive any moral rights by posting owned content.

text: You are responsible for any risks, damages, or losses that may incur by downloading materials
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users are responsible for any harm to their computer system or loss of data they may incur through the download of materials from the service.

text: You are free to choose your copyright license
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You have the right to choose or change the copyright license over content you publish through the Service.

text: Users agree not to submit illegal content
label: potentially_fair

text: You are solely responsible for claims made against the service and agree to indemnify and hold harmless the service.
label: potentially_fair
explanation:
You are responsible for your use of the service. If a claim arises related to that use, you agree to indemnify and hold the service harmless.
If users are also required to defend the service, see Case 146 instead.

text: The service can distribute your content through any media known now or in the future
label: potentially_fair

text: The service claims to be CCPA compliant for California users
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service has a different behavior towards users protected by the California Consumer Privacy Act.

text: The service uses your personal data for advertising
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Your interaction with the service and data you have shared with them may be gathered to create an advertising ID or profile to target ads on you, either on the website you were visiting or in third party websites.

text: The service will not allow third parties to access your personal information without a legal basis
label: clearly_fair

text: The service claims to be GDPR compliant for European users
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service has a different behavior towards users protected by the GDPR.

text: Pseudonyms are allowed
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You don't need to provide your full name to use the Service and you are allowed to use a pseudonym instead.

text: Usernames can be rejected or changed for any reason
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service's terms explain they may change or remove usernames at their sole discretion, and they may do so without prior notice.

text: Minors must have the authorization of their legal guardians to use the service
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Minors must have the authorization of their legal guardians to use the services. The legal guardians are responsible for the use of the service by the minor.

text: This service uses your personal information for many different purposes
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Personal Data can be processed for many reasons, including non essential processing such as for advertising, marketing and analytics purposes.

text: This service is only available for use individually and non-commercially.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Users can't use the Service for commercial purposes (unless the Service consents): it is only for personal, individual purposes.

text: This service cannot be held responsible for disputes that you may have with other users
label: potentially_fair
explanation: You are responsible for the interactions and the disputes that may occur with other users of the Service. In the event of a dispute, you release the Service from all claims resulting from it.

text: Users have a reduced time period to take legal action against the service
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: If you wish to bring legal action against a service about a past incident, you may not be able to do so if the Terms you agreed to implied a limited amount of time. As a result it may be worthy to carefully read that section of the Terms to know exactly how much time you might have before your right to take legal action expires.

text: Your personal data is given to third parties
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Your personal data is or may be given to third parties essential to the service’s operation. This could be an external spam detection service for an internet forum.

text: The service is not responsible for linked or (clearly) quoted content from third-party content providers
label: potentially_fair

text: You are allowed to quote their content with attribution
label: potentially_fair

text: The terms for this service are easy to read
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Agreements are well-organised, define legal terms (where applicable), aren't needlessly long and may be pleasant to read.

text: No logs are being kept
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Logs are text files containing some information about web visitors, such as user agents (showing for instance that you're connecting from Firefox for Android) and ip addresses (which reveal your approximate location). This case applies to Services that only keep the strictly necessary logs on their servers and therefore protect your privacy.

text: Content is published under a free license instead of a bilateral one
label: clearly_fair
explanation: User-generated content, such as posts or comments, is published under a free license, meaning other users can reuse it (e.g. Creative Commons license).

text: Usernames can be rejected for any reason
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service's terms explain they may change or remove usernames at their sole discretion, and they may do so without prior notice.

text: Instagram shares information with many third parties
label: potentially_unfair

text: Your identity is used in ads that are shown to other users
label: clearly_unfair
explanation:
Ads shown to other users may include a reference to you as an implied endorsement.
Might give away information about your own personal lifestyle to strangers.

text: Very broad copyright license on your content
label: potentially_unfair

text: Instagram Court of law is California
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction (specified in the title of each point by replacing the "X").

text: You waive your right to a class action
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The right to be represented in a class action (or representative action) exists in the US and some European countries. It allows people who have been similarly injured by the same accused to take legal action by a single lawsuit instead of individually. Some Terms of Service try to limit that right. By agreeing to them, you could waive (= renounce to) your right to a class action, which might reduce your chances to be successful in a case.

text: Tracking via third-party cookies for other purposes without your consent.
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service's website sets third-party cookies (cookies that do not belong to their domain) for purposes not including statistics and analytics.

text: This service can delete your content if you violate the terms
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Content infringing the Terms of Service (or the Acceptable Use Policy or equivalent)  may be removed, with or without notice.

text: The service provides a free help desk
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service can be easily contacted for questions or provides a support center at no extra cost.

text: The service refers users to external documents for more information
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service states that whenever it links to third party websites or applications, its privacy policy does not apply. Those third party websites or applications hosted or operated by other organizations may have their own privacy policies.

text: You can choose with whom you share content
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users can decide whether or not they want their content to be shared and they can control how and with whom it is shared.

text: This service keeps user logs for an undefined period of time
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Logs are text files containing some information about web visitors, such as user agents (showing for instance that you're connecting from Firefox for Android) and ip addresses (which reveal your approximate location). This case applies to Services that may keep logs indefinitely, allowing user sessions to be tracked over time. Logs that are kept for a time "consistent with applicable law" also fall under this case, since this period of time may be murky or undefined.

text: They store data on you even if you did not interact with the service
label: clearly_unfair
explanation: Some services store data on you even if you did not provide that data yourself. For example, users might provide access to their address book, after which a service might also store the phone numbers of that person's contacts.

text: This service is a subsidiary of Facebook
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service is a subsidiary of a certain company (called X in the title, to be replaced by the parent Company in each point's title). Company X’s Terms of Service may apply to the service.

text: Terms may be changed at their discretion, but they will notify you before.
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users will be notified a week before the Terms are somehow updated, which lets them a reasonable amount of time to carefully read the changes and perhaps stop using the services if they do not longer agree.

text: The service informs users about the risk of publishing personal info online
label: clearly_fair

text: Users are not allowed to use pseudonyms, as trust and transparency between users regarding their identities is relevant to the service.
label: potentially_fair
explanation: You must provide your legal name and pseudonyms aren't allowed, but it makes sense for the purposes of this service.

text: defend, indemnify, hold harmless; survives termination
label: potentially_fair

text: Facebook collects a lot of device information through the Instagram App.
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The app of the service requires more device permissions than necessary. As a consequence it can compromise your privacy by accessing many functions and information from your device, such as precise location, contacts, device identifiers etc.

text: Other applicable rules, terms, conditions or guidelines
label: potentially_fair
explanation: Use of the service is subject also to an exhaustive list of other documents containing rules, guidelines, terms or policies (in addition to the obvious Terms of Service/Terms of Use and Privacy Policy/Data Use Policy)

text: Conditions may change, but your continued acceptance is not inferred from an earlier acceptance
label: potentially_fair
explanation: When you're not logged in to a service, but use it repeatedly, it may still feel weird that their terms and conditions can change between one use and the next. However, this does not violate the rule-of-thumb that each new version should be accepted from scratch, without inferring your acceptance from the acceptance of an earlier version. Since no acceptance is inferred from continued use, but only from current use, we rate this as neutral instead of bad.

text: The service can delete your account  without a reason
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: At any time, your account can be terminated without explanation and without warning by the service.

text: The service notice you before closing your account
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Terms explain they will notify users at least 30 days before closing their accounts, which allows users to limit the losses and damages caused by this termination.

text: Apple.com can suspend the Site whenever they want, for any reason
label: potentially_fair
explanation: They may discontinue the services without prior notice, at any time, for any or no reason. They usually assume no liability for losses and damages resulting from this suspension.

text: The service gives 30 days of notice before closing your account
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Terms explain they will notify users at least 30 days before closing their accounts, which allows users to limit the losses and damages caused by this termination.

text: Any liability on behalf of the service is only limited to $ 50.00
label: potentially_unfair

text: Content you post may be edited by the service for any reason
label: clearly_unfair
explanation: This service can edit any content on the service for whatever the reason and at their sole discretion.

text: The service is only available in some countries approved by its government
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: Countries under embargo or banned by the government of the country in question cannot use the service.

text: This service assumes no responsibility and liability for the contents of links to other websites
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The service rejects any responsibility for the contents of other websites to which it links.

text: The court of law governing the terms is in Santa Clara County, California
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction (specified in the title of each point by replacing the "X").

text: When the service wants to change its terms, users are notified a month or more in advance
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service may update the Terms without notice, but if they significantly change the agreements in a manner that importantly and/or negatively affects the way the service is used, users will be notified at least 30 days in advance.

text: The service can delete specific content without reason and may do it without prior notice
label: clearly_unfair
explanation: This service can delete specific content. Usually they will claim they reserve the right to do it at their sole discretion, which is legalese for saying they won't give a specific reason. If the terms say they will not notify you, it shall be included in the quote but if it does not explicitly say they will give you a notice, it can be reasonably inferred that they can do it without telling you.

text: A complaint mechanism is provided for the handling of personal data
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The service provides users with contact information for an internal or external structure to lodge complaints. It also provides users with contact information for a higher data protection / privacy office in case the internal mechanism fails to satisfy the user.

text: If you offer suggestions to the service, they become the owner of the ideas that you give them
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The service can claim ownership on feedback, proposals, ideas and suggestions submitted by the users, who may waive their "moral rights" on that feedback.

text: Information is provided about security practices
label: clearly_fair
explanation: It's important to know what security measures are taken to protect your data from unauthorized access. This case applies to the Agreements that precisely explain their security practices.

text: Only temporary session cookies are used
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Temporary session cookies are cookies that are deleted when you close your browser instead of remaining there until they are manually deleted (or expire). These cookies are only used for the purposes of the service and therefore disappear when it's no longer used.

text: You are not being tracked
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The document excerpt needs to include the words "[the service] does not track [users]" or "[the service] does not use any tracking technology".

text: Third parties are not allowed to access your personal information without a legal basis
label: clearly_fair

text: This service is only available to users over 13 years of age
label: potentially_fair
explanation: The Services are intended for users who are at least a certain age. If you are younger than the age required then you are prohibited from using the Service.

text: The court of law governing the terms is in a jurisdiction (Nevada, USA) that is less friendly to user privacy protection
label: potentially_unfair
explanation: The Terms are governed by the applicable laws of a jurisdiction known for weak data regulations and poor user privacy protection. Usually contributors will need to explain in the Analysis view of each point linked to this Case the reason why it shall be applied.

text: Content removal from the service will be fair and proportionate.
label: clearly_fair
explanation: The Service promises to fairly terminate users accounts, only with a reason, and may give them an opportunity to explain and defend themselves.

text: Prohibits the publishing of personally identifiable information without the owner’s consent
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Users agree not to publicly post personally identifiable information (name, postal address, contact information…) without the owner’s knowledge or consent

text: Any liability on behalf of the service is only limited to the fees you paid as a user or $200
label: potentially_unfair

text: This service offers a symbolic but nonbinding statement about a matter of opinion, ethics, society, or politics
label: clearly_fair
explanation: If a service expresses a worldview antithetical to the user’s, that is a good signal that they should not use the service. Or conversely, if the service expresses an uncommonly-held belief that the user agrees with and thinks is relevant to the service-user relationship and/or important, they may wish to use that to choose between otherwise comparable services.

text: Users can scrape the site, as long as it doesn't impact the server too much
label: clearly_fair
explanation: Automated web scraping is a method of extracting information from a website. The service authorises scraping if it doesn't impact its server(s) too much.

text: Copyright license limited for the purposes of that same service but transferable and sublicenseable
label: clearly_fair

text: The service allows you to use pseudonyms
label: clearly_fair
explanation: You don't need to provide your full name to use the Service and you are allowed to use a pseudonym instead.

text: You cannot delete your contributions, but it makes sense for this service
label: potentially_fair
explanation: For services that are under an open license where your contributions are for public knowledge (such as Wikipedia and ToS;DR).

text: No third-party analytics or tracking platforms are used
label: clearly_fair

text: You sign away moral rights
label: clearly_unfair
explanation:
Moral rights are rights to creators of literary, dramatic, musical and artistic works, as well as directors of films.

"The moral rights include the right of attribution, the right to have a work published anonymously or pseudonymously, and the right to the integrity of the work. The preserving of the integrity of the work allows the author to object to alteration, distortion, or mutilation of the work that is "prejudicial to the author's honor or reputation". Anything else that may detract from the artist's relationship with the work even after it leaves the artist's possession or ownership may bring these moral rights into play. Moral rights are distinct from any economic rights tied to copyrights. Even if an artist has assigned his or her copyright rights to a work to a third party, he or she still maintains the moral rights to the work." -Wikipedia

In general, any terms that use the exact words "moral rights" as rights that are waived will fall under this Case.

text: The service does not retain logs of your activity after your session ends
